prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a black screen showing the logo text “bing & decide” in white and gold.

It then cuts to a close-up product shot: a black AXE body spray can standing on a wooden surface in a dimly lit room. The can appears to be an AXE Gold variant, with a yellow/gold label and “fresh” visible near the bottom. The background is blurred, with warm indoor lighting and indistinct objects.

Next, the scene shifts to a party-like interior with purple and pink lighting. A dartboard is mounted on a wall, seen from behind two out-of-focus people in the foreground. The dartboard is centered, suggesting attention is focused on it.

The following frames show a crowd of young adults gathered indoors, reacting excitedly. Many people are looking toward the center or off-screen, with open mouths, raised hands, and surprised or cheering expressions. The room has colorful party lighting, windows in the background, and a lively social atmosphere.

A man in the foreground wearing sunglasses leans backward dramatically while holding something near his mouth or face; the exact object is not clearly visible. People around him appear shocked, amused, or impressed, with several leaning in and reacting strongly.

The final frame overlays promotional text and product imagery on the party scene. Large text reads “SMELL FRESH ALL DAY.” Two AXE spray cans are shown on the right side, one black with a gold/yellow label and another darker can with a green label. The background still shows the crowd reacting behind the graphic overlay.
